title: Worktree Bootstrap Contract
non_product_work: Resolve the bootstrap gap where Bandit-created worktrees can be treated as runnable after claim and lock evidence without a repo-native bootstrap contract proving the environment is safe and ready for worker execution.
origin: The 2026-05-26 strategic review and 2026-05-27 Worktree Bootstrap Contract decision established that successful claim authority and `git worktree lock` evidence are not enough to make a worktree runnable. Bandit-created worktrees need a CLI-readable contract for copied or linked files, setup commands, validation command, environment-variable references, secret-handling boundaries, expected runtime dependencies, and bootstrap failure evidence before worker execution starts. `BANDIT-GAP-WORKTREE-BOOTSTRAP-CONTRACT` is now the first queued bootstrap gap after `BANDIT-050` resolved cockpit-status interstitial recovery.
scope: Define a repo-native Worktree Bootstrap Contract policy artifact, expected first at `.bandit/policy/worktree-bootstrap.json`, that records allowed copied or linked files, optional `.worktreeinclude`-style allow-list behavior, setup commands, validation command, environment-variable references, secret-handling boundary, expected runtime dependencies, and bootstrap failure evidence requirements.

Add focused RED evidence before implementation for policy validation, allowed copy/link entries, setup-command execution contract, validation-command execution contract, environment-reference validation, secret-copy refusal, bootstrap failure evidence, and worker-execution refusal while a Bandit-created worktree is locked but unbootstrapped.
Implement CLI-readable validation for the Worktree Bootstrap Contract without making `.bandit` projection files, generated caches, cockpit status, or worker prompts canonical workflow authority.

Define bootstrap execution behavior narrowly enough to prove setup and validation command wiring, environment reference checks, secret-copy refusal, and bootstrap failure evidence without starting scheduler execution or full parallel workstream orchestration.

Record bootstrap failure routing as PM-visible evidence that can later connect to claim failed, blocked, or recovery-required states without implementing claim release, cleanup, scheduler, or worker lifecycle authority in this chore.

Preserve the Git Mutation Serializer and Claim Authority boundaries: this chore may define runnable-worktree validation and refusal behavior, but it must not replace CAS claim authority, work-surface reservation logic, claim-owned worktree locking, or serializer-owned shared `.git` mutation behavior.
Record CLEAN_CODE.md read evidence in Stage 1; CLEAN_CODE.md was read on 2026-05-29 before creating this brief, and clean-code compliance must be evaluated before landing.

Stage capability scope: Codex PM owns Stage 1 brief creation, Stage 2 RED evidence, routine technical routing, and context-artifact synchronization; if Codex authors or materially edits Stage 2 RED tests, Stage 3 implementation is assigned to Claude through the bootstrap Process Adapter path; the Stage 3 Writer has no authority to edit tests, test helpers, fixtures, RED evidence, or acceptance mappings; CodeRabbit and Local Qwen own Stage 4 review evidence; Landing Agent owns Stage 5 verdict/action evidence.

Consume existing Clean-Code, Stage Rubric, Bootstrap Model-Family Separation, Test Ownership Boundary, Operator Fail-Closed Boundary, Input Quarantine Gate, Layered Risk Classification, Supply-Chain Gate, Coordination Event Log Authority, CAS Claim Authority, Git Mutation Serializer, Claim-Owned Worktree Lock, and review-subject-hash vocabulary without redefining those gates.

Future-work scope: this chore must not implement event-driven scheduler execution, full worktree lifecycle enablement, true parallel writable workstream enablement, claim lease creation or release, claim authority changes, work surface reservations, automatic merge/push/deploy behavior, product UAT approval, cockpit UI/server/API work, state-index persistence, actor identity policy, PR/CI workflow execution, live reviewer routing changes, paid reviewer routes, external service integration, installed global skill edits, dependency or lockfile changes, or unrelated Phase 8 work.
acceptance_criteria: The chore brief exists at docs/work/<ID>/brief.md and links to BANDIT-GAP-WORKTREE-BOOTSTRAP-CONTRACT.

Stage 1 brief evidence records CLEAN_CODE.md read evidence, Stage capability scope, Codex-owned technical decisions, source hierarchy, Worktree Bootstrap Contract boundary, secret-handling boundary, Bootstrap Model-Family Separation, Test Ownership Boundary, Operator Fail-Closed Boundary, Git Mutation Serializer boundary, and Claim Authority boundary.
Focused RED evidence proves Bandit currently lacks an enforced Worktree Bootstrap Contract before a created or locked worktree can be treated as runnable.

The Worktree Bootstrap Contract policy artifact validates allowed copied or linked files, optional allow-list declarations, setup commands, validation command, environment-variable references, expected runtime dependencies, and bootstrap failure evidence requirements.
Secret material is not copied into worktrees by default; any direct secret-copy entry is refused unless existing operator-supervised policy explicitly authorizes a narrower exception.
Environment-variable requirements are references to approved sources or required names, not secret values embedded in policy, generated evidence, command output, or worktree bootstrap artifacts.

Bootstrap setup and validation command execution records enough evidence for Codex PM to tell whether a worktree is runnable, failed, blocked, or recovery-required, while keeping worker prompts and cockpit views non-canonical.
Worker execution is refused when a Bandit-created worktree is locked but Worktree Bootstrap Contract validation has not passed.

Bootstrap failure evidence is durable and PM-visible, and it is shaped so future claim failure, blocked, or recovery-required routing can consume it without guessing from chat or transient terminal output.

The implementation preserves the Git Mutation Serializer boundary for shared `.git` worktree mutations and does not introduce direct worktree add, remove, prune, lock, unlock, branch/ref maintenance, or packed-refs-affecting behavior outside that serializer.
The implementation preserves CAS claim authority and does not treat `.bandit` files, filesystem locks, worktree markers, cockpit status, or bootstrap validation output as writable claim authority.

Templates, validation, or derived status surfaces identify the Worktree Bootstrap Contract as required before runnable-worktree status without making cockpit state, cached status, or generated projections authoritative.
Stage 4 review evidence uses pre-PR CodeRabbit and Local Qwen at the current review subject hash unless honest provider refusal or bootstrap-gap evidence is recorded.

Layered risk-classification and supply-chain gate evidence are recorded before landing because this chore touches workflow gates, policy validation, generated schemas or templates, command routing, and worktree bootstrap behavior.
Clean-code compliance is evaluated before landing; any accepted non-blocking concern becomes a tagged follow-up or explicit no-action decision.
BANDIT-GAP-WORKTREE-BOOTSTRAP-CONTRACT is resolved or explicitly dispositioned in .bandit/bootstrap-gaps.json only after landing action and retrospective closeout evidence exist.

No event-driven scheduler execution, full worktree lifecycle enablement, true parallel writable workstream enablement, claim lease creation or release, work-surface reservation implementation, automatic merge/push/deploy behavior, product UAT approval, cockpit UI/server/API work, state-index persistence, actor identity policy, PR/CI workflow execution, live reviewer routing change, paid reviewer route, external service integration, installed global skill edit, dependency or lockfile change, or unrelated Phase 8 work is introduced.
verification_plan: Run focused Worktree Bootstrap Contract RED/GREEN tests for policy parsing, required fields, allowed copy/link declarations, optional allow-list support, setup-command execution contract, validation-command execution contract, environment-reference validation, expected runtime dependency validation, secret-copy refusal, bootstrap failure evidence, and worker-execution refusal before bootstrap passes.
Run focused tests proving a locked but unbootstrapped Bandit-created worktree is not considered runnable.
Run focused tests proving bootstrap failure evidence can route to failed, blocked, or recovery-required states without executing claim release, cleanup, scheduler, or worker lifecycle authority.
Run focused tests proving Worktree Bootstrap Contract validation does not become claim authority and does not bypass the Git Mutation Serializer for shared `.git` worktree mutations.
Run node --test test/worktree-bootstrap.test.mjs.
Run node --test test/validate.test.mjs if repo validation behavior is touched.
Run node --test test/cockpit-status.test.mjs if derived status exposes Worktree Bootstrap Contract gate state.
Run node --test test/work-item-create.test.mjs if work-item brief rendering or parsing is touched.

Run npm test if implementation touches shared command routing, validators, artifact renderers, work item parsing, templates, bootstrap gaps, risk classification, supply-chain gates, claim authority, git mutation policy, cockpit status, or policy validation beyond focused tests.
Run npm run typecheck.
Run npm run bandit -- validate.
Run npm run bandit -- gaps list.
Run node ./bin/bandit.mjs cockpit status --json.
Run node ./bin/bandit.mjs session-context current --json.
Run npm run bandit -- git-mutation validate --json if worktree-related shared `.git` mutation policy is touched.
Run npm run bandit -- claim validate --json if claim authority, claim state, fencing token, or claim projection behavior is touched.

Run npm run bandit -- supply-chain-gate validate --json because worktree bootstrap policy can affect copied or linked files, generated schemas or templates, executable commands, and external tool-install surfaces.
Run npm run bandit -- risk-classification validate --json.
Run npm run bandit -- input-quarantine validate --json.
Run npm run bandit -- operator-boundary validate --json.
Run npm run bandit -- coordination-authority validate --json.
Run node ./bin/bandit.mjs review-subject-hash <ID> for aggregate review evidence freshness.
Run npm run bandit -- coderabbit-review pre-pr <ID> --base origin/main before Stage 4 closeout, unless provider refusal evidence is recorded.
Run npm run bandit -- qwen-review <ID> before Stage 4 closeout.
Run npm run bandit -- land-check <ID> before landing.
Run git diff --check.
operator_input_status: No operator-owned input is required before creating this bootstrap-gap chore or writing RED evidence. Repo artifacts identify the queued gap, source artifacts, accepted Worktree Bootstrap Contract decision, current Stage Rubric requirements, Clean-Code authority, Git Mutation Serializer boundary, Claim Authority boundary, Operator Fail-Closed Boundary, and Codex PM boundary that worktree bootstrap mechanics are routine technical decisions when repo evidence and policy are sufficient. Halt only if implementation would change product direction, UAT policy, workflow policy beyond enforcing the accepted worktree bootstrap gap, business tradeoffs, explicit cost/risk posture, secret-copy exception policy, external service setup, paid reviewer spend approval, paid reviewer routing, live routing, scheduler authority, claim authority, worktree lifecycle authority beyond bootstrap validation, installed global skill contents, dependency or lockfile policy, merge/push/deploy authority, or broader workflow scope.
